channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19186] sanity test_103a: setfacl: Operation not supported on RHEL 9.6 server
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/a6f03f48-7d8e-4cbc-8aa1-9c4eede6353a

test_103a failed with the following error:

[31] $ setfacl -R -m u:bin:rwx h -- failed
~                                     ? setfacl: h: Operation not supported    
~                                     ? setfacl: h/x: Operation not supported

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/115124 - 5.14.0-503.40.1.el9_5.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/115124 - 5.14.0-570.24.1_lustre.el9.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity test_103a - run_acl_subtest 'cp' failed
item.summary: sanity test_103a: setfacl: Operation not supported on RHEL 9.6 server

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Merged for 2.17

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61834/
Subject: LU-19186 osd-ldiskfs: use posix ACL api
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 35f58473619c265abac4ed98dc1092dd5afada32

"Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61834
Subject: LU-19186 osd-ldiskfs: use posix ACL api
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 3ff6f316e09cc5db4ccc8c3e416c94b2d9f22b1c

Hi Jian,

Sorry for the late reply. Indeed I remember I had been buzzed by ACL handling with newer kernels. I had pushed patches
"LU-18095 sec: fix ACL handling on recent kernels" https://review.whamcloud.com/56254
and
"LU-18101 sec: fix ACL handling on recent kernels again" https://review.whamcloud.com/56552
to address this. Purpose of the second patch was to support the new .get_inode_acl op on struct inode_operations. Looking at the build output for el9.6, I can see this op is properly detected, so it should be used on client side.

Now, on server side, I can see ldiskfs defines a handler for .get_inode_acl but not for .get_acl.

const struct inode_operations ldiskfs_file_inode_operations = {
	.setattr	= ldiskfs_setattr,
	.getattr	= ldiskfs_file_getattr,
	.listxattr	= ldiskfs_listxattr,
	.get_inode_acl	= ldiskfs_get_acl,
	.set_acl	= ldiskfs_set_acl,
	.fiemap		= ldiskfs_fiemap,
	.fileattr_get	= ldiskfs_fileattr_get,
	.fileattr_set	= ldiskfs_fileattr_set,
};

const struct inode_operations ldiskfs_dir_inode_operations = {
	.create		= ldiskfs_create,
	.lookup		= ldiskfs_lookup,
	.link		= ldiskfs_link,
	.unlink		= ldiskfs_unlink,
	.symlink	= ldiskfs_symlink,
	.mkdir		= ldiskfs_mkdir,
	.rmdir		= ldiskfs_rmdir,
	.mknod		= ldiskfs_mknod,
	.tmpfile	= ldiskfs_tmpfile,
	.rename		= ldiskfs_rename2,
	.setattr	= ldiskfs_setattr,
	.getattr	= ldiskfs_getattr,
	.listxattr	= ldiskfs_listxattr,
	.get_inode_acl	= ldiskfs_get_acl,
	.set_acl	= ldiskfs_set_acl,
	.fiemap         = ldiskfs_fiemap,
	.fileattr_get	= ldiskfs_fileattr_get,
	.fileattr_set	= ldiskfs_fileattr_set,
};

const struct inode_operations ldiskfs_special_inode_operations = {
	.setattr	= ldiskfs_setattr,
	.getattr	= ldiskfs_getattr,
	.listxattr	= ldiskfs_listxattr,
	.get_inode_acl	= ldiskfs_get_acl,
	.set_acl	= ldiskfs_set_acl,
};

So I am wondering, do you see if ldiskfs_get_acl is called on server side? The client calls md_getxattr, and maybe this is the origin of the problem? We would need a new md_getacl call so that when it is processed on server side, the acl specific handler is called instead of the xattr one? How does it work when fetching ACLs for a file or directory on ext4 or ldiskfs on el9.6, can you trace which functions in the stack get called?

The same issue also occurred on RHEL 10.0 server testing.
Hi sebastien, could you please advise?

Hi sebastien,
I saw you worked on https://review.whamcloud.com/56332 (LU-18095 sec: fix ACL handling on recent kernels) before. Could you please take a look at the ACL issue from RHEL 9.6 kernel in this ticket? Thank you.

Hi adilger,
I found calling ext4_xattr_{get,set}() directly in osd_xattr_{get,set,del}() to bypass the is_posix_acl_xattr() check didn't work properly.
While running setfacl, getfacl, and cp, etc. operations, the return values from ext4_xattr_{get,set}() are different from those in RHEL 9.5 server.
Attached is the patch of kernel commit v6.1-rc1-24-g318e66856dde: xattr-use-posix-acl-api.patch
There are more POSIX ACL changes in RHEL 9.6 kernel.

I'm updating https://review.whamcloud.com/58222 to restore ll_vfs_removexattr() calling in osd_xattr_del() to see how sanity test goes.

The previously failed sanity/43a, 43b, 350 passed now.
However, the following two subtests still failed:

== sanity test complete, duration 18076 sec ============== 14:17:50 (1755267470)
sanity: FAIL: test_103a run_acl_subtest 'cp' failed
sanity: FAIL: test_103e Not all default ACLs were inherited: 0 != 2

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/56ea86f3-01ab-442a-9019-53198001f8f9

[3] $ umask 022 – ok
[4] $ mkdir d – ok
[5] $ cd d – ok
[6] $ touch f – ok
[7] $ setfacl -m u:bin:rw f – ok
[8] $ ls -l f | awk – '{ print $1 }' – ok
[11] $ cp f g – ok
[12] $ ls -l g | awk – '{sub(/\./, "", $1); print $1 }' – ok
[15] $ rm g – ok
[16] $ cp -p f g – ok
[17] $ ls -l f | awk – '{ print $1 }\' – ok
[20] $ mkdir h – ok
[21] $ echo blubb > h/x – ok
[22] $ cp -rp h i – ok
[23] $ cat i/x – ok
[26] $ rm -r i – ok
[31] $ setfacl -R -m u:bin:rwx h – ok
[32] $ getfacl --omit-header h/x – ok
[40] $ cp -rp h i – failed
~ ? cp: preserving permissions for 'i': Operation not supported

Copying a directory (without setting ACL) passed, however, copying a directory (with setting ACL) failed with -EOPNOTSUPP returned from ll_vfs_removexattr().
So, restoring ll_vfs_removexattr() calling in osd_xattr_del() can fix the sanity/43a, 43b, 350 test failures but still hit the -EOPNOTSUPP issue in sanity/103a.

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60762
Subject: LU-19186 kernel: debug posix acl xattr issue
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 037f9a11d2b6dee9f8351aa45906e97365359e4c

cp: preserving permissions for '/mnt/lustre/d43a.sanity/sleep': No data available

The -ENODATA was returned from directly calling ext4_xattr_set() in osd_xattr_del().
However, I found the cp operation passed with the original ll_vfs_removexattr() calling in osd_xattr_del(). Although it returned -EOPNOTSUPP, cp operation considered it as pass:

# strace -ttt cp -p /usr/bin/sleep /mnt/lustre/dir/sleep

1755243197.740287 fgetxattr(3, "system.posix_acl_access", 0x7fff1f85c070, 132) = -1 ENODATA (No data available)
1755243197.740465 fstat(3,
Unknown macro: {st_mode=S_IFREG|0755, st_size=36496, ...}
) = 0
1755243197.740646 fsetxattr(4, "system.posix_acl_access", "\2\0\0\0\1\0\7\0\377\377\377\377\4\0\5\0\377\377\377\377 \0\5\0\377\377\377\377", 28, 0) = -1 EOPNOTSUPP (Operation not supported)
1755243197.742832 fchmod(4, 0100755) = 0
1755243197.743273 close(4) = 0
1755243197.743523 close(3) = 0
1755243197.743569 munmap(0x7efdfb5fe000, 4202496) = 0
1755243197.743660 lseek(0, 0, SEEK_CUR) = -1 ESPIPE (Illegal seek)
1755243197.743711 close(0) = 0
1755243197.743753 close(1) = 0
1755243197.743791 close(2) = 0
1755243197.743880 exit_group(0) = ?
1755243197.744047 +++ exited with 0 +++

I'm updating https://review.whamcloud.com/58222 to restore ll_vfs_removexattr() calling in osd_xattr_del() to see how sanity test goes.
My guess is that cp is returning the error directly from ext4_xattr_get() when it tries to read the ACL xattr, but the kernel wrappers are hiding this error?

After exporting and calling ext4_xattr_{get,set}() in https://review.whamcloud.com/58222, a new issue occurred:

striped dir -i3 -c2 -H all_char /mnt/lustre/d43a.sanity
cp: preserving permissions for '/mnt/lustre/d43a.sanity/sleep': No data available
 sanity test_43a: @@@@@@ FAIL: can't copy

Investigating further.
Another option would be to add EXPORT_SYMBOL(ext4_xattr_get/set()) so that osd-ldiskfs can call these functions directly.

In RHEL 9.6 kernel 5.14.0-570.30.1.el9_6:

__vfs_getxattr()
ssize_t
__vfs_getxattr(struct dentry *dentry, struct inode *inode, const char *name,
               void *value, size_t size)
{
        const struct xattr_handler *handler;

        if (is_posix_acl_xattr(name))
                return -EOPNOTSUPP;

        handler = xattr_resolve_name(inode, &name);
        if (IS_ERR(handler))
                return PTR_ERR(handler);
        if (!handler->get)
                return -EOPNOTSUPP;
        return handler->get(handler, dentry, inode, name, value, size);
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL(__vfs_getxattr);

ftrace showed that:

lod_xattr_get() {
  lu_context_key_get();
  osd_xattr_get() {
    lu_context_key_get();
    __vfs_getxattr() {
      xattr_resolve_name();
      ldiskfs_xattr_trusted_get() {
        ldiskfs_xattr_get() {

        // ......

}

It seems we cannot call handler->get() (which calls ext4_xattr_get()) from osd_xattr_get() to bypass __vfs_getxattr().

Thank you adilger for the suggestions.

Instead, _osd_xattr_get/set() could be calling inode>i_op->get/setxattr() methods of the underlying ext4 code directly, which would avoid this check.

The inode>i_op->get/setxattr() methods have been removed from struct inode_operations since kernel v4.8:

commit fd50ecaddf8372a1d96e0daeaac0f93cf04e4d42
Author:     Andreas Gruenbacher <agruenba@redhat.com>
AuthorDate: Thu Sep 29 17:48:45 2016 +0200
Commit:     Al Viro <viro@zeniv.linux.org.uk>
CommitDate: Fri Oct 7 21:48:36 2016 -0400
    
    vfs: Remove {get,set,remove}xattr inode operations
    
    These inode operations are no longer used; remove them.

commit 97d2116708ca0fd6ad8b00811ee4349b7e19e96f
Merge: 30066ce675d3 fd50ecaddf83 
Author:     Linus Torvalds <torvalds@linux-foundation.org>
AuthorDate: Mon Oct 10 17:11:50 2016 -0700
Commit:     Linus Torvalds <torvalds@linux-foundation.org>
CommitDate: Mon Oct 10 17:11:50 2016 -0700
    
    Merge branch 'work.xattr' of git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/viro/vfs
      
    Pull vfs xattr updates from Al Viro:
     "xattr stuff from Andreas
      
      This completes the switch to xattr_handler ->get()/->set() from
      ->getxattr/->setxattr/->removexattr"
      
    * 'work.xattr' of git://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/viro/vfs:
      vfs: Remove {get,set,remove}xattr inode operations
      xattr: Stop calling {get,set,remove}xattr inode operations
      vfs: Check for the IOP_XATTR flag in listxattr
      xattr: Add __vfs_{get,set,remove}xattr helpers
      libfs: Use IOP_XATTR flag for empty directory handling
      vfs: Use IOP_XATTR flag for bad-inode handling
      vfs: Add IOP_XATTR inode operations flag
      vfs: Move xattr_resolve_name to the front of fs/xattr.c
      ecryptfs: Switch to generic xattr handlers
      sockfs: Get rid of getxattr iop 
      sockfs: getxattr: Fail with -EOPNOTSUPP for invalid attribute names
      kernfs: Switch to generic xattr handlers
      hfs: Switch to generic xattr handlers
      jffs2: Remove jffs2_{get,set,remove}xattr macros
      xattr: Remove unnecessary NULL attribute name check

So, we cannot call inode>i_op->get/setxattr() methods.

The osd-ldiskfs _osd_xattr_get/set() functions are calling ll_vfs_get/setxattr()>vfs_get/stxattr() to hide kernel API changes, but we don't strictly need to do this. Instead, _osd_xattr_get/set() could be calling inode>i_op->get/setxattr() methods of the underlying ext4 code directly, which would avoid this check.

This might need to revert parts of patch https://review.whamcloud.com/38175 ("LU-13430 vfs: add ll_vfs_getxattr/ll_vfs_setxattr compat macro") that change over to using __vfs_get/setxattr() and instead put the version compat checks directly into ll_vfs_get/setxattr(). It may be necessary to add the CAP_SYS_ADMIN check into osd-ldiskfs for accessing trusted.* xattrs, since the check in ext4_xattr_trusted_list() would now be skipped. I checked the methods in ext4_xattr_handlers[] and they don't really do anything but call ext4_xattr_get/set() directly.

While running setfacl, ll_set_acl() will set name either with XATTR_NAME_POSIX_ACL_ACCESS or XATTR_NAME_POSIX_ACL_DEFAULT, which will make the new __vfs_getxattr() in RHEL 9.6 kernel always return "-EOPNOTSUPP".

ll_set_acl()
        switch (type) {
        case ACL_TYPE_ACCESS:
                name = XATTR_NAME_POSIX_ACL_ACCESS;
                break;
        
        case ACL_TYPE_DEFAULT:
                name = XATTR_NAME_POSIX_ACL_DEFAULT;
                if (!S_ISDIR(inode->i_mode)) 
                        rc = acl ? -EACCES : 0;
                break;

        default:
                rc = -EINVAL;
                break;
        }

Hi adilger, could you please advise how to resolve this issue?

It turned out the "-EOPNOTSUPP" error was returned from __vfs_getxattr() in RHEL 9.6 kernel 5.14.0-570.30.1.el9_6:

__vfs_getxattr()
ssize_t
__vfs_getxattr(struct dentry *dentry, struct inode *inode, const char *name,
               void *value, size_t size)
{
        const struct xattr_handler *handler;

        if (is_posix_acl_xattr(name))
                return -EOPNOTSUPP;

        // ......
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL(__vfs_getxattr);

The is_posix_acl_xattr() is defined in linux/xattr.h as follows:

is_posix_acl_xattr()
static inline bool is_posix_acl_xattr(const char *name)
{
        return (strcmp(name, XATTR_NAME_POSIX_ACL_ACCESS) == 0) ||
               (strcmp(name, XATTR_NAME_POSIX_ACL_DEFAULT) == 0);
}

The above changes were added by the following kernel commit:

commit 318e66856ddec05384f32d60b5598128289f4e7b
Author:     Christian Brauner <brauner@kernel.org>
AuthorDate: Thu Sep 22 17:17:22 2022 +0200
Commit:     Christian Brauner (Microsoft) <brauner@kernel.org>
CommitDate: Thu Oct 20 10:13:31 2022 +0200

    xattr: use posix acl api
        
    In previous patches we built a new posix api solely around get and set
    inode operations. Now that we have all the pieces in place we can switch
    the system calls and the vfs over to only rely on this api when
    interacting with posix acls. This finally removes all type unsafety and
    type conversion issues explained in detail in [1] that we aim to get rid
    of.
    
    With the new posix acl api we immediately translate into an appropriate
    kernel internal struct posix_acl format both when getting and setting
    posix acls. This is a stark contrast to before were we hacked unsafe raw
    values into the uapi struct that was stored in a void pointer relying
    and having filesystems and security modules hack around in the uapi
    struct as well. 
    
    Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20220801145520.1532837-1-brauner@kernel.org [1]
    Signed-off-by: Christian Brauner (Microsoft) <brauner@kernel.org>

# setfacl -R -m u:sanityusr:rwx /mnt/lustre/d125.sanity
setfacl: /mnt/lustre/d125.sanity: Operation not supported

Debug log on Client:

(client.c:1482:ptlrpc_check_status()) lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff88e635ec1800: operation mds_getxattr to node 10.240.26.174@tcp failed: rc = -95

Debug log on MDS:

(lod_object.c:1551:lod_xattr_get()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551521 : -95 : ffffffffffffffa1)

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60440
Subject: LU-19186 tests: debug setfacl failure
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: f70b4714e27d50c45aa73fd3dfca00f176516aa0

The same issue also made other subtests fail:

sanity: FAIL: test_103a run_acl_subtest 'cp' failed
sanity: FAIL: test_103e Able to add just 2 default ACLs
sanity: FAIL: test_103f Cannot add default ACLs
sanity: FAIL: test_125 setfacl /mnt/lustre/d125.sanity failed
sanity: FAIL: test_154a setfacl /mnt/lustre/.lustre/fid/[0x200003ab2:0x22:0x0] failed
sanity: FAIL: test_154b setfacl /mnt/lustre/.lustre/fid/[0x240003ab2:0x3:0x0] failed

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/a6f03f48-7d8e-4cbc-8aa1-9c4eede6353a
